approved_candidate.pipeline_status: DCC source candidate; geometry approved for later Step 14 planning

macro_geometry_findings.finding: Approved view-owned piecewise-linear faceted envelope and back/right holdout invariants remain intact. Planar facets are bounded approved interpretation, not an unapproved smoothing defect.

Independent masonry-course envelopes are distinct, watertight, positively overlapped, and visually separated. Individual stone divisions remain later Normal/Base Color consumers except approved source-critical silhouette breaks.
The apron preserves N3/K80 containment, front/left source-owned inward refinements, and the approved 32-column macro transition. Micro-rubble is deferred.

Blade motif, inscriptions, fissures, stone grain, microchips, individual-stone surface divisions, and appearance response require Step 14 planning. Their absence neither fails geometry nor constitutes finished appearance.

The 220 cm tapered monolith and three-level base remain a readable chunky Blood Axe cairnstone silhouette. The 8,000-triangle value is a target rather than a minimum; the 784-triangle source remains below the hard cap without forcing texture-owned detail into geometry.

review_package_history.evidence: The immutable Step 12 top proof touched both horizontal image boundaries because 154 cm vertical orthographic scale implied only 126 cm horizontal span at 900 by 1100 for a 140 cm apron.

review_package_history.correction: Rerender six Step 13 proof-only fixed-camera views from the unchanged blend; top orthographic scale changed only in memory to 190 cm.

review_evidence.board_role: presentation thumbnails only; native comparisons are the evidence

explicitly_deferred_to_step14_planning: ORM, AO, roughness, metallic, filtering, bake, and emissive decisions
next_authority: after mandatory restart, preparation only of a separate Step 14 UV, Base Color, and Material-Interpretation Plan contract
